heading: The hybrid model elements
subtitle: What you described through your feedback is not a compromise between options. It is a model in its own right. The combination of elements you asked for appears consistently across clubs, staff, and committees.

items.title: Shared services
items.description: Clubs will have the option to move administrative, compliance, and coordination functions to SLSNZ. Volunteers are freed from back-office burden and able to focus on what brought them to the movement.

items.title: Club Partner Network
items.description: A network of  Club Partners provides each club with a single, trusted point of contact. They simplify engagement with SLSNZ, provide proactive support, and connect clubs to the right expertise when they need it.

items.title: Centrally coordinated national service lines
items.description: Specialist support, including training coordination, qualifications, sport, lifesaving, and governance, is coordinated nationally and delivered consistently across all clubs regardless of location or regional capacity.

items.title: Clearer club facing roles with defined accountability
items.description: Club-facing roles have defined responsibilities, clear expectations, and explicit accountability. Clubs know who to contact, what to expect, and how decisions are made.

items.title: Service catalog
items.description: A clear guide to every support service SLSNZ offers, including how to access it and who to contact. No more trying to work out who to call.

items.title: Online self-service tools
items.description: Digital tools that allow clubs to find information, complete common tasks, and resolve queries independently. Reduces reliance on staff for routine matters.

items.title: Structured inter-club collaboration
items.description: Facilitated opportunities for clubs to connect, share knowledge, and learn from each other. Organised around shared context so the right clubs are talking to the right people.
